target.title: feat(workboard): add move command to CLI and workboard_move tool
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Workboard agents and operators can create, claim, and complete cards, but there was no direct way to move a card from one column (status) to another. Agents had to rely on the dispatch command or manual store mutations to transition cards, which is awkward for deliberate column changes like "moving a card to Review" or "sending it back to Triage." Why This Change Was Made The store already had a move(id, status, position) method, but it was never exposed to the CLI or the agent tools layer. This change wires it up in both directions: вЂў CLI: workboard move --status for operators and agents running in the terminal вЂў Tool: workboard_move(id, status, token?) for agent tool use, with claim-scoped access control (only agents that have claimed the card can move it, matching the pattern used by reassign and release) Status values are validated against the WORKBOARD_STATUSES enum at the tool layer before reaching the store, so invalid statuses are rejected early with a clear error message. User Impact вЂў Agents can now move cards between columns as part of their workflow (e.g., moving a card to review after completing implementation) вЂў Operators can manually move cards via the CLI without writing store queries вЂў Both CLI and tool outputs support --json for machine-readable consumption I want to underline that the code was written with AI-assistance. But I went over the code and checked it manually ## Evidence The following evidence comes from manually conducting terminal runs. I am limiting the outputs to the relevant lines. First I create a new card $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard create card-abc123 --status tod acc31be5 todo normal default card-abc123 Here is a list to show the status on the board before moving a card $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard list acc31be5 todo normal default card-abc123 OK, now the moving comes: $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard move acc31be5 --status running acc31be5 running normal default card-abc123 List again to see: $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard list acc31be5 running normal default card-abc123 Show details: $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard show acc31be5 acc31be5 running normal default card-abc123 Try moving again: $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard move acc31be5 --status review acc31be5 review normal default card-abc123 Try moving back. With JSON: $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard move acc31be5 --status triage --json { "card": { "id": "acc31be5-2e01-4dd0-93a9-5999779d9a30", "title": "card-abc123", "status": "triage", "priority": "normal", "labels": [], "position": 1000, "createdAt": 1782332679684, "updatedAt": 1782333474509, "startedAt": 1782333120149, "events": [ { "id": "efb13f78-423f-4467-a443-6d61881f8433", "kind": "created", "at": 1782332679684, "toStatus": "todo" }, { "id": "df656eb0-3c22-4998-b3db-934e3931836b", "kind": "moved", "at": 1782333120149, "fromStatus": "todo", "toStatus": "running" }, { "id": "e9446e73-521a-41b8-bffe-16c8a9efde59", "kind": "moved", "at": 1782333379120, "fromStatus": "running", "toStatus": "review" }, { "id": "1f72ee1f-3736-4269-9c95-b6411a08a7db", "at": 1782333474509, "kind": "moved", "fromStatus": "review "toStatus": "triage" } ] } } What happens if the status is not valid? $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard move acc31be5 --status nonexistant Error: status must be one of: triage, backlog, todo, scheduled, ready, running, review, blocked, done. Exactly, we want a reasoned error if the column does not exist. What if the --status flag is missing? $ pnpm openclaw --dev workboard move acc31be5 Error: --status is required. Use: workboard move --status This is also correct: a status is necessary to move the card to a target column. Tests were also conducted: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/workboard/src/tools.test.ts extensions/workboard/src/cli.test.ts RUN v4.1.8 /home/clawdbot/openclaw вњ“ extensions extensions/workboard/src/tools.test.ts (7 tests) 350ms вњ“ extensions extensions/workboard/src/cli.test.ts (10 tests) 55ms Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 17 passed (17) There is no linked issue for this PR. ## Update 1: All fixes are implemented and committed. Here's the summary: Changes Made (4 commits): feat(workboard): add move command to CLI and workboard_move tool вЂ” Initial implementation fix(workboard): add claim-scope enforcement and canonical status validation to move вЂ” P1 fixes: вЂў Added workboard_move to plugin registration names list (index.ts) вЂў Moved claim-scope enforcement into store.move() method (using assertCanMutateClaimedCard) вЂў Used canonical WORKBOARD_STATUSES enum in CLI instead of hardcoded list вЂў Passed scope through tools layer to store for proper ownership checks 3. test(workboard): add claim scope enforcement test for store.move() вЂ” Test proving claim scope works test(workboard): add canonical status validation test вЂ” Test proving all 8 valid statuses work via canonical enum Test Results: 19 tests pass (12 CLI + 7 tools) The claim scope test proves that: вЂў Agent-b cannot move a card claimed by agent-a (throws "card is claimed by agent-a") вЂў Agent-a can move its own claimed card (succeeds) The canonical status test proves all 8 valid statuses work through the CLI using the shared enum. Using node version v24.17.0 on ubuntu 24.04.4 Proof image of the passed tests added: ## update 2: ### additional real behavior proof $ git rev-parse --short HEAD a2e886d649 $ pnpm openclaw --version $ node scripts/run-node.mjs --version [openclaw] Building TypeScript (dist is stale: config_newer - config newer than build stamp). [openclaw] Building bundled plugin assets.
